_comment: Example config for the Calendar Agent (TASK-29). Copy this to ~/.config/agent-manager/calendar-agent.json and replace every <PLACEHOLDER>. JSON has no comments, so explanatory keys are prefixed with _comment. They are ignored by loadConfig(). Full step-by-step setup: see docs/SETUP.md.
_comment_mcpServers: Three MCP servers the host connects. Calendar + Gmail are the OFFICIAL Google remote MCP servers (type: http) — the host injects a `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>` header at startup from the Google OAuth token manager (see google-oauth.example.json + docs/SETUP.md). Do NOT put client_id/secret/token here. WhatsApp is the local lharries/whatsapp-mcp stdio server and MUST already be running + authenticated before the agent starts.

mcpServers._comment_calendar: Official Google Calendar remote MCP (Developer Preview). google:true tells the host to inject the bearer token; scopes are informational (the OAuth grant must already include them). Our agent READS all calendars and WRITES only the AI calendar (boundary enforced by the prompt, see TASK-30) → it needs the calendar.events WRITE scope plus read scopes.
mcpServers._comment_gmail: Official Gmail remote MCP (Developer Preview). Our agent only READS Gmail → gmail.readonly. (If the official Gmail MCP rejects readonly at live connect, widen the scope — see docs/SETUP.md.)
mcpServers._comment_whatsapp: lharries/whatsapp-mcp Python server, launched via uv. The Go bridge (whatsapp-bridge/main.go) runs SEPARATELY and must be running + QR-authenticated first. Paths below are the real verified ones for this machine.

_comment_whitelist: Filters inputs BEFORE the agent reasons (AC#3). WhatsApp groups are matched by name (case-insensitive). Gmail passes if the From address contains any sender substring OR the message carries an allowed label. Empty arrays = deny-all. Replace the placeholders with your real values.
